topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the lecture hall seemed to burn directly into my skin. Every glance from Professor Albright felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. When the accusation was finally made, the air in the room thickened, becoming stagnant and suffocating. I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles were white, the cheap plastic digging painfully into my palms. My mouth went dry, and I could feel a flush creeping up my neck, staining my cheeks a painful red. I kept my gaze fixed on the scuffed linoleum floor, focusing intently on a single, microscopic speck of dust, desperately trying to make myself invisible.

[story 2]

"You didn't do this, did you?" The question hung in the air, sharp and accusatory. I could feel the heat rising in my face, a sudden, intense warmth that spread from my collarbone straight up to my hairline. My hands started to tremble uncontrollably, and I instinctively wanted to pull my arms across my body as a shield. Every sound—the rustle of papers, the distant tapping of a pen—seemed amplified, dissecting my silence. Internally, a tight knot formed in my stomach, a churning anxiety that made my throat constrict. I just wanted the floor to swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The silence following the announcement was deafening, a vast, echoing void where my confidence used to reside. I could feel my entire body tense, every muscle coiled tight, ready to spring away. When the committee members started speaking, their voices were measured and cold, each word landing like a tiny stone in still water. I stared at my notes, the ink blurring into meaningless shapes. A strange, hollow sensation settled over me, a profound sense of exposure. I shifted in my seat, a small, jerky movement, wishing with every fiber of my being that the chair would simply dissolve beneath me.
